title: What Happens Immediately After a Death at Home
summary: In the first minutes after a death at home, there are only a few things that actually need to happen right away — everything else can wait.
category: Death of a Loved One (Transition into After-a-Loss)
journeyStage: Death of a Loved One (Transition into After-a-Loss)
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: What Happens Immediately After a Death at Home | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: In the first minutes after a death at home, there are only a few things that actually need to happen right away — everything else can wait.
whatYouNeedToKnow: If death was expected (hospice, terminal illness), you generally do not need to call 911.
The hospice team or a funeral home can guide the very next steps.

paragraphs: If the death was expected — under hospice care, or after a long illness — you typically do not need to call 911. Instead, call the hospice team (they're reachable 24/7) or the person's doctor, who can pronounce the death. If the death was sudden or unexpected, call 911.

Once the immediate call is made, there's genuinely nothing else that must happen in that exact moment. Sitting with what's just happened, calling a close family member, or simply taking a few minutes before making the next call are all reasonable.

nextSteps: If on hospice, call the hospice team first.
Call one close family member or friend before tackling anything else.
disclaimer: If you're reading this in the moments after a loss, we're sorry for what you're going through. There's no need to have everything figured out right now.

sources.name: National Hospice and Palliative Care Organization
